candidates.text: A Memphis restaurant owner kicked out National Guard members and had every legal right to do it. Fine. But that does not mean it was right, and it certainly does not mean he gets to cry about the response. If customers want to boycott the restaurant, hammer it with bad reviews, and take their business somewhere else, that is the consequence of the choice he made. You cannot flex your right to refuse service and then complain when other people exercise their right to respond. He decided to cho...

candidates.text: The worst part of The Odyssey isn't the bad costumes or the woke casting. It's honestly the writing. Christopher Nolan is a talented director, but as a screenwriter, he fails to grasp an important concept: Show, don't tell. The dialogue in the film is basically an unending rehashing of these lines: Penelope: I want Odysseus! These suitors are annoying! Telemachus: Where's my dad? He's coming back! Instead of these characters just repeating these concepts again and again, the film would have b...

candidates.text: If only there were another epic story about human frailty and/or the dangers of technology, one that contained moments of spectacle as well as intimate emotion. It would be great if it had never been adapted. Maybe he should check out what big novels were published in, say…1996

candidates.text: I have seen a few minutes of Dune: Part 3 & I truly can’t process how fucking good this movie is going to be. The battle for the Oscar for Best Picture between Dune & The Odyssey might be the most competitive Best Picture battle of our lives. We will tell our grandchildren about this year. The film looks horrifying & utterly stunning. What a cherry on-top of a great day.
